question: According to the Lumina Accords, what is the designated purpose of 'quiet zones'?

answers.ground_truth: The designated purpose of 'quiet zones' is to provide a sensory-neutral space or refuge for species with hypersensitive abilities.
answers.original: I do not have enough information to answer.
answers.perturbed: Quiet zones are designated sensory-neutral spaces in shared habitats for species with hypersensitive abilities.

ops.description: Memory-Welding requires intense concentration and focus, a skill possessed by Mind-Smiths.

entities.name: Lumina Accords
entities.description: The Lumina Accords are a set of agreements signed after a conflict between two major federations, containing 27 articles governing trade, diplomacy, and cohabitation.

entities.name: Article 12
entities.description: Article 12 of the Lumina Accords details regulations for atmospheric composition.

entities.name: Pan-Galactic Council
entities.description: The Pan-Galactic Council adjudicates violations of the Lumina Accords.

entities.name: Standard Habitat Construction Materials
entities.description: Standard habitat construction materials must be non-toxic to all member species.

relations.description: The Pan-Galactic Council adjudicates violations of the Lumina Accords.
The Lumina Accords mandate that habitat construction materials must be non-toxic to all member species.
Each shared habitat must have its charter ratified by an Accords representative.
Article 12 of the Lumina Accords details regulations for atmospheric composition.
The Lumina Accords provide legal protection for species like the Cygnans.
